messaging.label: Messaging
messaging.prompt: How do you talk to friends and family most of the time?
messaging.rec: Move sensitive conversations to Signal, SimpleX or Session.

o0.label: WhatsApp / Messenger / SMS
o0.hint: Metadata and centralised platforms stay the weak point.

o1.label: Telegram for most conversations
o1.hint: Better than the big platforms, but the privacy is still limited.

o2.label: Signal or Molly for most chats
o2.hint: Already a solid base for censorship resistance.

o3.label: SimpleX / Session / Matrix for sensitive use
o3.hint: Less dependence on a phone number and a central platform.

email.label: Email
email.prompt: Which service do you use most for your email?
email.rec: Use Proton Mail or Tuta, and add PGP for the contacts that matter.

o0.label: Gmail / Outlook / iCloud Mail
o0.hint: Storage and content access stay heavily centralised.

o1.label: Proton Mail or Tuta for everyday use
o1.hint: Already a stronger option for privacy.

o2.label: Proton / Tuta + PGP for some contacts
o2.hint: A good level of resistance if you use it consistently.

o3.label: Regular PGP / OpenPGP with careful metadata habits

browser.label: Browser & search
browser.prompt: Which browser and search engine do you use most?
browser.rec: Drop Chrome + Google for a privacy-respecting browser and search engine.

o1.label: Firefox or Brave with a private search engine
o1.hint: A real step forward for privacy.

o3.label: Mullvad Browser / LibreWolf / Tor Browser by context

dns.label: DNS
dns.prompt: How do you resolve domain names?
dns.rec: Switch to encrypted DNS with a minimal, privacy-first resolver.

o0.label: Your ISP or router DNS
o0.hint: The easiest option, but often the least private.

o1.label: Encrypted DNS from a mainstream provider

o2.label: Quad9 / Mullvad DNS / NextDNS
o2.hint: A real improvement if you want to limit traceability.

o3.label: Encrypted DNS plus a deliberate anti-tracking strategy
o3.hint: A more deliberate and robust posture.

vpn.label: VPN
vpn.prompt: What kind of VPN do you use?
vpn.rec: Use a reputable no-log VPN such as Mullvad, Proton VPN or IVPN.

o0.label: No VPN
o0.hint: The most exposed case for network traceability.

o1.label: A free or dubious VPN
o1.hint: Can be worse than none if the provider is opaque.

o2.label: A reputable no-log VPN with a clear policy

o3.label: Mullvad / Proton VPN / IVPN for regular use
o3.hint: A more serious and coherent posture.

passwords.label: Passwords
passwords.prompt: How do you manage your passwords?
passwords.rec: Adopt a password manager with unique, long passwords everywhere.

o0.label: Reused passwords or saved in the browser
o0.hint: High risk in a breach or account compromise.

o1.label: A basic password manager
o1.hint: Already a very good step forward.

o2.label: Bitwarden / KeePassXC / Proton Pass
o2.hint: A healthy posture that is simple to maintain.

o3.label: Password manager + unique, long passwords
o3.hint: The most robust way to reduce damage from a breach.

twofa.label: Two-factor auth
twofa.prompt: What level of two-factor authentication do you use?
twofa.rec: Move 2FA to an authenticator app or, better, a hardware key.

o0.label: No 2FA
o0.hint: The weakest level of account protection.

o1.label: SMS or phone call
o1.hint: Better than nothing, but very fragile.

o2.label: TOTP in an app (Aegis / Ente Auth)

storage.label: Storage
storage.prompt: Where do you store your sensitive files?
storage.rec: Move sensitive files to Proton Drive, Cryptomator or encrypted local storage.

o1.label: Proton Drive or a basic encrypted storage service

o3.label: Local storage + self-hosting / advanced encryption
o3.hint: The most serious level for sovereignty.

social.label: Social
social.prompt: Where do you spend most of your social life online?
social.rec: Anchor your presence on decentralised networks and lower platform dependence.

o0.label: Meta / X / big platforms only
o0.hint: The simplest, but the most exposed to pressure.

o2.label: A mix of decentralised networks and direct contact

o3.label: Alternative networks and low platform dependence
o3.hint: That is the true logic of resistance.

os.label: Operating system
os.prompt: What operating system do you use most?
os.rec: Move toward GrapheneOS on mobile and Linux on the desktop.

o0.label: Stock Android / iOS / Windows
o0.hint: Very convenient, but much less privacy-respecting.

o1.label: A more private Android / Linux / macOS

tor.label: Tor & anonymity
tor.prompt: How much do you use Tor / Tails / anonymity tools?
tor.rec: Build a Tor (and, when needed, Tails) habit for your most sensitive tasks.

o0.label: Almost never
o0.hint: Very exposed if the threat ever becomes serious.

o1.label: Occasionally, without much discipline
o1.hint: A first step, but still fragile.

o3.label: Tor + Tails with strong discipline
o3.hint: The most suitable level for a high-threat context.

money.label: Money
money.prompt: How do you handle your digital money?
money.rec: Add privacy-aware payment options and separate identities where you can.

o0.label: Bank cards and standard accounts only

o2.label: A mix of private accounts and limited crypto use

exposed.title: Exposed
exposed.summary: Your setup is still heavily tied to the big platforms. The first steps are simple and high-impact.

awakening.title: Awakening
awakening.summary: You have started to diversify your tools. A few deliberate changes could make a real difference.

resistant.title: Resistant
resistant.summary: Your setup is already serious. You have a solid base of censorship and surveillance resistance.

hardened.title: Hardened
hardened.summary: You run a hardened stack with a strong pseudonymous baseline. The next focus is discipline and compartmentalisation.

sovereign.title: Sovereign
sovereign.summary: Your posture is already very strong. You are close to high digital sovereignty, with demanding technical choices.
